topic: aeration of young structured red wine
trigger_keywords: vino joven con alto contenido fenólico al ser aireado
vinos jóvenes con alta estructura tánica

steps.label: cause
steps.text: A young, phenolic, tightly aromatic red wine is exposed deliberately to air before service.

steps.label: mechanism
steps.text: Mixing with air increases the release of volatile compounds and can dissipate some closed or reductive characters; it does not instantly remove tannin.

steps.label: effect
steps.text: The wine can show greater aromatic expression and seem more open, while its underlying tannic structure remains.

steps.label: exam_formulation
steps.text: Formative causal formulation: Vigorous aeration can open a young structured wine aromatically without instantly eliminating its tannins.

_meta.source_note: Inferred formative model - not official WSET assessment or mark-scheme content
